task_completion.description: Verify that a Maven project named 'gs-maven' is created in Eclipse.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Package Explorer or Project Explorer shows a project folder named 'gs-maven'. It should ideally have a small 'M' icon on the folder indicating it is recognized as a Maven project.

task_completion.description: Verify the pom.xml contains the correct groupId, artifactId, and joda-time dependency.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The pom.xml file is open in the Eclipse editor, showing <groupId>org.springframework</groupId>, <artifactId>gs-maven</artifactId>, and a <dependency> block for 'joda-time'.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct package structure and file placement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Package Explorer shows the 'src/main/java' directory expanded, containing a package named 'hello'. Inside the 'hello' package, there are two files: 'Greeter.java' and 'HelloWorld.java'.
Greeter.java is open in the Eclipse editor, showing a class named 'Greeter' with a method named 'sayHello()' that returns a String greeting (e.g., 'Hello world!').

task_completion.description: Verify the implementation of HelloWorld.java using joda-time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: HelloWorld.java is open in the Eclipse editor, showing a 'main' method. The code must import a class from joda-time (e.g., org.joda.time.LocalTime) and use it to get and print the current time.

The Eclipse Console view shows a successful Maven build message (e.g., 'BUILD SUCCESS') or the successful execution output of the HelloWorld program (printing the time and greeting). The Eclipse Problems view should not show any red compilation errors for these files.

integrity.description: Verify that the Eclipse IDE was used to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Eclipse IDE interface being actively used to create the project, edit files, and run the build, rather than the agent exclusively using a terminal window or a basic text editor.

integrity.description: Verify that the build or execution output is genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Console shows standard Java/Maven execution output formatting. There should be no evidence of the agent manually typing the expected output or using a script to simply echo the success messages.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply clone the completed source repository.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen creating the project, creating the packages, and writing/pasting the code in Eclipse, rather than executing 'git clone https://github.com/spring-attic/gs-maven' in a terminal and importing the finished project.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: The expected Maven groupId is 'org.springframework' and artifactId is 'gs-maven'. The project must include a joda-time dependency in its pom.xml. The source repository is https://github.com/spring-attic/gs-maven. Greeter.java should have a sayHello() method returning a greeting, and HelloWorld.java must use the joda-time library to display the current time.
